QF.id: Terminology in Equity and Debt Markets_33

question.text: The Replacement Value of a firm refers to:

answer.text: The price at which shares are currently trading
The market value of all assets at today's cost to recreate the entire infrastructure
The face value of all shares issued
The liquidation value after paying all debts

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Replacement value is what it would cost to set up a new company with all the infrastructure that an existing company has. It represents the current cost of recreating the firm's assets. **The Trap:** This is different from market value (share price), face value (nominal), or liquidation value (after debts). **Cross-Exam Context:** Replacement value is discussed in NISM Series XV Chapter 3 as one of the measures of value. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When market cap is significantly below replacement value, it may signal deep undervaluation — it is cheaper to buy the company than to build it from scratch.
